title: PerformanceMetrics.Calculate accumulates stale state across calls

evidence.quote:
pm.GrossProfit = decimal.ZERO
	pm.GrossLoss = decimal.ZERO
	pm.TotalProfit = decimal.ZERO
	pm.AverageWinPct = decimal.ZERO
	pm.AverageLossPct = decimal.ZERO

	if len(trades) == 0 {
		return
	}

	pm.TotalTrades = len(trades)

pm := NewPerformanceMetrics()
	pm.Calculate(trades, equityCurve, decimal.New(10000), decimal.New(10420), 252)

reasoning: Calculate only clears a small subset of fields before recomputing, then increments WinningTrades and LosingTrades from their prior values. Reusing the same PerformanceMetrics instance for another calculation can produce impossible results, such as TotalTrades being reset to the new slice length while WinningTrades/LosingTrades still include previous runs. The empty-trades early return also leaves most previous metrics intact.
recommendation: At the start of Calculate, reset all derived metric fields before any early return while preserving configuration such as RiskFreeRate, then assign the new inputs and compute from scratch.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Every included test creates a fresh NewPerformanceMetrics instance before a single Calculate call, and TestEmptyTrades also starts from a fresh instance, so stale state after reuse is never exercised.
suggestedRegressionTest: Call Calculate twice on the same PerformanceMetrics instance with different trade sets, including an empty second set, and assert all counts and derived metrics reflect only the latest inputs.
